These claims directly negate each other within the same document. rationale.R1.2: The delegation is exclusively 'check logical consistency,' making this load-bearing. The artifact contains at least two severe internal contradictions that must be surfaced. (1) Supervised/unsupervised contradiction: the Data Collection section describes training on 12,400 human-annotated labels with a written rubric, yet the Deployment Scope section asserts ROUTERSCAN is 'fully unsupervised: it requires no annotated examples … and no ground-truth labels of any kind.' These claims directly negate each other within the same document. (2) AUC-to-prevalence non-sequitur: the Results section states 'From this AUC [0.91], we therefore conclude that the rate of anomalous routing decisions … is approximately 18 percent.' AUC measures rank-discrimination ability, not prevalence; the 18.2% figure was fixed by the annotation protocol in Data Collection, not inferred from AUC. The stated causal chain ('From this AUC … we conclude [prevalence]') is logically invalid. Both failures are cross-section coherence problems: premises established in one section are contradicted or misused in another.
